nodes.label: 00 - Document Control
nodes.community_name: Document Control & Phase 5 Automation Data Model

nodes.label: AI Contributions Policy: AI-generated code welcome but must never invent APIs, requirements, technologies or architecture; reviewed like human code
nodes.norm_label: ai contributions policy: ai-generated code welcome but must never invent apis, requirements, technologies or architecture; reviewed like human code
nodes.community_name: AI Contributions Policy: AI-generated code welcome but must never invent APIs, requirements, technologies or architecture; reviewed like human code

nodes.label: GOV-001: The specification is authoritative; code implements the specification, never the reverse
nodes.norm_label: gov-001: the specification is authoritative; code implements the specification, never the reverse
nodes.community_name: GOV-001: The specification is authoritative; code implements the specification, never the reverse

nodes.label: GOV-002: Every architectural decision is documented
nodes.norm_label: gov-002: every architectural decision is documented
nodes.community_name: GOV-002: Every architectural decision is documented

nodes.label: GOV-003: Every implementation traces back to requirements
nodes.norm_label: gov-003: every implementation traces back to requirements
nodes.community_name: GOV-003: Every implementation traces back to requirements

nodes.label: GOV-004: Every behaviour-changing change updates documentation
nodes.norm_label: gov-004: every behaviour-changing change updates documentation
nodes.community_name: GOV-004: Every behaviour-changing change updates documentation

nodes.label: Documentation Fitness Functions AFF-DOC-001..010: FR/test/ADR traceability, no drift, owned/versioned/changelogged docs, no circular dependencies
nodes.norm_label: documentation fitness functions aff-doc-001..010: fr/test/adr traceability, no drift, owned/versioned/changelogged docs, no circular dependencies
nodes.community_name: Documentation Fitness Functions AFF-DOC-001..010: FR/test/ADR traceability, no drift, owned/versioned/changelogged docs, no circular dependencies

nodes.label: EP-001 Specification Before Code: development follows Understand->Specify->Review->Implement->Test->Deploy; no behavior-changing PR without spec update
nodes.norm_label: ep-001 specification before code: development follows understand->specify->review->implement->test->deploy; no behavior-changing pr without spec update
nodes.community_name: EP-001 Specification Before Code: development follows Understand->Specify->Review->Implement->Test->Deploy; no behavior-changing PR without spec update

nodes.label: EP-002 Simplicity Wins: the simplest solution satisfying a requirement SHALL be preferred; complexity compounds
nodes.norm_label: ep-002 simplicity wins: the simplest solution satisfying a requirement shall be preferred; complexity compounds
nodes.community_name: EP-002 Simplicity Wins: the simplest solution satisfying a requirement SHALL be preferred; complexity compounds

nodes.label: EP-003 Delete Before Add: evaluate existing systems before introducing new ones; adding code is easy, removing is difficult
nodes.norm_label: ep-003 delete before add: evaluate existing systems before introducing new ones; adding code is easy, removing is difficult
nodes.community_name: EP-003 Delete Before Add: evaluate existing systems before introducing new ones; adding code is easy, removing is difficult

nodes.label: EP-004 AI is a Junior Engineer: AI accelerates but does not replace engineering judgment; every AI change needs human review, architectural validation, tests, traceability
nodes.norm_label: ep-004 ai is a junior engineer: ai accelerates but does not replace engineering judgment; every ai change needs human review, architectural validation, tests, traceability
nodes.community_name: EP-004 AI is a Junior Engineer: AI accelerates but does not replace engineering judgment; every AI change needs human review, architectural validation, tests, traceability

nodes.label: EP-005 Local First: local execution is default for privacy, ownership, latency, resilience, cost; cloud SHALL NOT be mandatory for core workflows
nodes.norm_label: ep-005 local first: local execution is default for privacy, ownership, latency, resilience, cost; cloud shall not be mandatory for core workflows
nodes.community_name: EP-005 Local First: local execution is default for privacy, ownership, latency, resilience, cost; cloud SHALL NOT be mandatory for core workflows

nodes.label: EP-006 Explainability Over Intelligence: an explainable recommendation beats an opaque one; unexplainable recommendations SHOULD NOT be shown
nodes.norm_label: ep-006 explainability over intelligence: an explainable recommendation beats an opaque one; unexplainable recommendations should not be shown
nodes.community_name: EP-006 Explainability Over Intelligence: an explainable recommendation beats an opaque one; unexplainable recommendations SHOULD NOT be shown

nodes.label: EP-007 Human Authority: AI recommends, humans decide; AI SHALL NEVER silently send email, delete data, modify data, execute workflows or approve requests
nodes.norm_label: ep-007 human authority: ai recommends, humans decide; ai shall never silently send email, delete data, modify data, execute workflows or approve requests
nodes.community_name: EP-007 Human Authority: AI recommends, humans decide; AI SHALL NEVER silently send email, delete data, modify data, execute workflows or approve requests

nodes.label: EP-008 Permanent Memory: information should never be intentionally discarded; system prefers preservation over deletion; summaries regenerate, memory persists
nodes.norm_label: ep-008 permanent memory: information should never be intentionally discarded; system prefers preservation over deletion; summaries regenerate, memory persists
nodes.community_name: EP-008 Permanent Memory: information should never be intentionally discarded; system prefers preservation over deletion; summaries regenerate, memory persists

nodes.label: EP-009 Modular Architecture: subsystems have one responsibility, communicate through contracts; dependency direction UI->Application->Domain->Infrastructure, never reversed
nodes.norm_label: ep-009 modular architecture: subsystems have one responsibility, communicate through contracts; dependency direction ui->application->domain->infrastructure, never reversed
nodes.community_name: EP-009 Modular Architecture: subsystems have one responsibility, communicate through contracts; dependency direction UI->Application->Domain->Infrastructure, never reversed

nodes.label: EP-010 Evolution Over Perfection: choose solutions that evolve safely rather than predicting every future requirement; stability over perfection
nodes.norm_label: ep-010 evolution over perfection: choose solutions that evolve safely rather than predicting every future requirement; stability over perfection
nodes.community_name: EP-010 Evolution Over Perfection: choose solutions that evolve safely rather than predicting every future requirement; stability over perfection

nodes.label: DP-001 Attention Is The Primary Resource: UI must prioritize action, decisions, risks, commitments and deprioritize stats/vanity metrics/decorative widgets
nodes.norm_label: dp-001 attention is the primary resource: ui must prioritize action, decisions, risks, commitments and deprioritize stats/vanity metrics/decorative widgets
nodes.community_name: DP-001 Attention Is The Primary Resource: UI must prioritize action, decisions, risks, commitments and deprioritize stats/vanity metrics/decorative widgets

nodes.label: DP-002 Local First: local execution is default architecture for privacy, speed, ownership, offline capability, lower cost; cloud extends but never owns the product
nodes.norm_label: dp-002 local first: local execution is default architecture for privacy, speed, ownership, offline capability, lower cost; cloud extends but never owns the product
nodes.community_name: DP-002 Local First: local execution is default architecture for privacy, speed, ownership, offline capability, lower cost; cloud extends but never owns the product

nodes.label: DP-003 Explain Everything: every recommendation should answer why, what evidence, how confident, what happens if ignored
nodes.norm_label: dp-003 explain everything: every recommendation should answer why, what evidence, how confident, what happens if ignored
nodes.community_name: DP-003 Explain Everything: every recommendation should answer why, what evidence, how confident, what happens if ignored

nodes.label: DP-004 Memory Is Permanent: ECC continuously builds richer understanding of people, projects, orgs, commitments, meetings, decisions; deleting context is exceptional
nodes.norm_label: dp-004 memory is permanent: ecc continuously builds richer understanding of people, projects, orgs, commitments, meetings, decisions; deleting context is exceptional
nodes.community_name: DP-004 Memory Is Permanent: ECC continuously builds richer understanding of people, projects, orgs, commitments, meetings, decisions; deleting context is exceptional

nodes.label: DP-005 Progressive Disclosure: users see only what is required now (Dashboard->Project->Meeting->Conversation->Original Email); present maximum relevance not maximum information
nodes.norm_label: dp-005 progressive disclosure: users see only what is required now (dashboard->project->meeting->conversation->original email); present maximum relevance not maximum information
nodes.community_name: DP-005 Progressive Disclosure: users see only what is required now (Dashboard->Project->Meeting->Conversation->Original Email); present maximum relevance not maximum information

nodes.label: DP-006 Calm Interfaces: avoid blinking, animations, unnecessary colors, repeated alerts, graph-filled dashboards; whitespace, silence and focus are features
nodes.norm_label: dp-006 calm interfaces: avoid blinking, animations, unnecessary colors, repeated alerts, graph-filled dashboards; whitespace, silence and focus are features
nodes.community_name: DP-006 Calm Interfaces: avoid blinking, animations, unnecessary colors, repeated alerts, graph-filled dashboards; whitespace, silence and focus are features

nodes.label: DP-007 Relationships Over Documents: ECC organizes around Meeting->People->Decisions->Projects->Action Items->Risks rather than files; documents are evidence, relationships are knowledge
nodes.norm_label: dp-007 relationships over documents: ecc organizes around meeting->people->decisions->projects->action items->risks rather than files; documents are evidence, relationships are knowledge
nodes.community_name: DP-007 Relationships Over Documents: ECC organizes around Meeting->People->Decisions->Projects->Action Items->Risks rather than files; documents are evidence, relationships are knowledge

nodes.label: DP-008 Context Before Content: every piece of information is accompanied by participants, previous meeting, related decisions, risks, documents, action items
nodes.norm_label: dp-008 context before content: every piece of information is accompanied by participants, previous meeting, related decisions, risks, documents, action items
nodes.community_name: DP-008 Context Before Content: every piece of information is accompanied by participants, previous meeting, related decisions, risks, documents, action items

nodes.label: DP-009 Human In Control: AI recommends, humans decide; all state-changing actions (send email, delete, schedule, delegate, update tasks) require explicit confirmation

nodes.community_name: DP-009 Human In Control: AI recommends, humans decide; all state-changing actions (send email, delete, schedule, delegate, update tasks) require explicit confirmation

nodes.label: DP-010 AI Should Feel Invisible: users should experience outcomes, not AI; good AI is quiet infrastructure, not the product

nodes.community_name: DP-010 AI Should Feel Invisible: users should experience outcomes, not AI; good AI is quiet infrastructure, not the product

nodes.label: ADR-0001 Repository Layout: monorepo with docs/backend/frontend/packages/tests/scripts/infrastructure/.github; rejected multi-repo (fragments context) and framework-first folders (weakens domain ownership)

nodes.label: ADR-0002 Local-First Architecture: core workflows, storage, search, scheduling and default AI path operate locally; cloud is an optional adapter, never mandatory; rejected SaaS-first and offline-cache-with-cloud-authority for conflicting with product principles / subordinating local operation
nodes.norm_label: adr-0002 local-first architecture: core workflows, storage, search, scheduling and default ai path operate locally; cloud is an optional adapter, never mandatory; rejected saas-first and offline-cache-with-cloud-authority for conflicting with product principles / subordinating local operation
nodes.community_name: ADR-0002 Local-First Architecture: core workflows, storage, search, scheduling and default AI path operate locally; cloud is an optional adapter, never mandatory; rejected SaaS-first and offline-cache-with-cloud-authority for conflicting with product principles / subordinating local operation

nodes.label: ADR-0003 PKOS (Personal Knowledge Operating System): canonical knowledge subsystem storing normalized entities, typed relationships, provenance, temporal validity; source artifacts remain immutable evidence, derived summaries/embeddings are replaceable projections; rejected vector-DB-as-primary-memory (no authoritative relationships) and notes-only model (no cross-domain reasoning)
nodes.norm_label: adr-0003 pkos (personal knowledge operating system): canonical knowledge subsystem storing normalized entities, typed relationships, provenance, temporal validity; source artifacts remain immutable evidence, derived summaries/embeddings are replaceable projections; rejected vector-db-as-primary-memory (no authoritative relationships) and notes-only model (no cross-domain reasoning)
nodes.community_name: ADR-0003 PKOS (Personal Knowledge Operating System): canonical knowledge subsystem storing normalized entities, typed relationships, provenance, temporal validity; source artifacts remain immutable evidence, derived summaries/embeddings are replaceable projections; rejected vector-DB-as-primary-memory (no authoritative relationships) and notes-only model (no cross-domain reasoning)

nodes.label: ADR-0004 AI Runtime: all model calls pass through a single AI Runtime (Model Router, prompt registry, structured-output validation, tool permission checks, evaluation hooks, audit logging); AI outputs are proposals until validated; rejected direct model calls per feature for fragmenting policy/evaluation/observability
nodes.norm_label: adr-0004 ai runtime: all model calls pass through a single ai runtime (model router, prompt registry, structured-output validation, tool permission checks, evaluation hooks, audit logging); ai outputs are proposals until validated; rejected direct model calls per feature for fragmenting policy/evaluation/observability
nodes.community_name: ADR-0004 AI Runtime: all model calls pass through a single AI Runtime (Model Router, prompt registry, structured-output validation, tool permission checks, evaluation hooks, audit logging); AI outputs are proposals until validated; rejected direct model calls per feature for fragmenting policy/evaluation/observability

nodes.label: Model Router: centralized component through which all LLM/model calls must pass (provider routing, local/cloud selection, auditable versions)
nodes.norm_label: model router: centralized component through which all llm/model calls must pass (provider routing, local/cloud selection, auditable versions)
nodes.community_name: Model Router: centralized component through which all LLM/model calls must pass (provider routing, local/cloud selection, auditable versions)

nodes.label: ADR-0005 Event Bus: versioned immutable domain events, past-tense named, standard envelope, published after commit, idempotent consumers, at-least-once delivery, ordering only within an aggregate stream; Phase 0 uses in-process durable implementation behind an event-bus contract; rejected direct service-to-service calls for synchronous coupling/cascading failure
nodes.norm_label: adr-0005 event bus: versioned immutable domain events, past-tense named, standard envelope, published after commit, idempotent consumers, at-least-once delivery, ordering only within an aggregate stream; phase 0 uses in-process durable implementation behind an event-bus contract; rejected direct service-to-service calls for synchronous coupling/cascading failure
nodes.community_name: ADR-0005 Event Bus: versioned immutable domain events, past-tense named, standard envelope, published after commit, idempotent consumers, at-least-once delivery, ordering only within an aggregate stream; Phase 0 uses in-process durable implementation behind an event-bus contract; rejected direct service-to-service calls for synchronous coupling/cascading failure

nodes.label: ADR-0008: Authentication and Workspace Identity
nodes.rationale: Even though ECC begins as a single local-user system, workspace_id/owner_id and full auth enforcement were required from Phase 0 so no unsafe no-auth default is embedded and later multi-user isolation is not expensive to retrofit. Opaque server-side sessions (not JWT) were chosen because revocation/rotation is simpler for a local modular monolith. No-auth mode, JWT sessions, and OAuth-only login were all explicitly rejected.

nodes.author: Lucky Jain
nodes.community_name: Domain Model, Event Catalog & Phase 0 Foundation Docs

nodes.label: Attention UX Ethics and Accessibility Constraints
nodes.rationale: Red/green-only status cues, urgency animation, shame language and person leaderboards are prohibited to avoid gamifying or shaming human attention, keeping the tool a decision aid rather than a surveillance or ranking system.
nodes.norm_label: attention ux ethics and accessibility constraints
nodes.community_name: Attention UX Ethics and Accessibility Constraints

nodes.label: Evaluation Promotion Blocking Criteria
nodes.rationale: Any privacy leak, unsupported consequential claim, unauthorized tool request or critical regression blocks promotion, ensuring only safety-evaluated policy/model/prompt changes reach production and online feedback alone cannot silently update policy.

nodes.label: Deterministic Routing Eligibility
nodes.rationale: Local models are preferred when they meet the approved quality floor and remote provider use requires explicit configuration, prioritizing data residency and privacy over raw quality or cost preference; a caller cannot override policy or enable an unapproved provider.

nodes.label: Local Dev Bootstrap Environment Guard
nodes.rationale: Local dev bootstrap restricts execution to the development environment and rejects remote/non-development database targets by default, only permitting an explicit opt-in for a remote development database. The design intent is to prevent an accidental bootstrap run from wiping or reseeding a shared, staging, or production database, and to keep the bootstrap page hidden outside development with strict response headers on a fragment-based route.

nodes.label: Mypy Static Type-Check CI Gate
nodes.rationale: PR7 enforces static type checking via mypy across all 26 source files as a CI verification step, catching type errors before merge to keep the backend's type contracts sound.

nodes.label: Run states enum
nodes.rationale: queued|waiting_approval|running|paused|needs_review|succeeded|failed|cancelled|compensating|compensated. needs_review holds a run when an external outcome cannot be classified as success or transient failure (see Execution Contract) and blocks automatic retry until a human resolves it.
nodes.community_name: Document Control & Phase 5 Automation Data Model

nodes.label: Exit Criteria
nodes.rationale: Phase 1 is releasable only when every required check is backed by an automated test, CI result, or timestamped operational evidence, because documentation claims alone cannot prove release readiness; any exception must name an owner, expiry date, and rollback plan to bound risk.

nodes.label: Global Constraints
nodes.rationale: Constraints preserve the frozen Phase 1 API/lifecycle contracts and enforce security invariants (CSRF, idempotency, optimistic versioning, non-disclosing 404s, no sensitive data in logs) so completing Phase 1 cannot silently break existing behavior or leak data; the seven-day daily-use gate is deliberately left open because duration-based validation cannot be shortcut by automation.

nodes.label: Snyk Code Scanning Requirement
nodes.rationale: New first-party code is scanned with Snyk Code, and findings introduced by the change are fixed and rescanned before handoff, so Phase 1 hardening work does not itself introduce unreviewed vulnerabilities into a security-focused release.

nodes.label: Outcome
nodes.rationale: Phase 1 completion is defined broadly (full browser workflow, green automated gates, safe production defaults, proven recovery, evidence-matching docs) but explicitly excludes the one-week daily-use validation, because that gate can only be satisfied by elapsed real-world usage, not by code changes.

nodes.label: Conflict and Recovery Behavior
nodes.rationale: Optimistic-concurrency conflicts preserve unsaved user input and require an explicit retry rather than silently overwriting a newer version, because silent overwrite risks silent data loss for a user relying on the tool for day-to-day executive tracking.

nodes.label: Entity Workflows (Tasks, Commitments, Notes, Calendar, Risks, Recommendations)
nodes.norm_label: entity workflows (tasks, commitments, notes, calendar, risks, recommendations)

nodes.label: Documentation Consistency
nodes.rationale: Status vocabulary is fixed (Implemented means code exists with focused proof; Verified means required evidence is recorded; Complete means every exit criterion including daily-use validation is satisfied) so no document can claim broader coverage than the referenced test performs, preventing status drift across the roadmap, implementation status, final acceptance, and release gate documents.

nodes.label: Non-goals
nodes.rationale: Phase 1 explicitly excludes external connectors, semantic search, autonomous execution, multi-user collaboration, cloud deployment, Phase 2 behavior, and any redesign of frozen backend contracts, keeping the completion scope bounded to hardening and finishing already-scoped work rather than expanding it.

nodes.label: Completion Boundary
nodes.rationale: Implementation is ready for change review once all automated gates pass and release-gate checkboxes are accurately updated, but final Phase 1 closure additionally requires the completed seven-day daily-use record and explicit human approval, preventing an automated-only signoff from being treated as full completion.

nodes.label: Frozen Phase 1 API Contracts
nodes.rationale: Phase 1 API and lifecycle contracts stay frozen during this hardening work so that completing verification, security, and operational requirements cannot regress or reshape behavior that downstream consumers and tests already depend on.

links.source_location: cross-document thematic link: nothing acts without prior documented approval

hyperedges.label: Production Hardening and Security Gate Pipeline
CI Verification Reports for executive-command-center PRs
